title: Requirements for Accreditation of Philippine Overseas Shipping Enterprise with Philippine Flagship
content:
The Philippine Overseas Shipping Enterprise (POSE) with Philippine flagship shall submit the following documents for accreditation:

A. Owned tonnage:
1. Letter request of the ship owner including:
   i. Confirmation that the company owns the ship for enrollment;
   ii. Former name of the enterprise (if any);
   iii. Ex-name of the ship (if any).
2. Valid certificate of accreditation issued by MARINA confirming the company as an accredited maritime enterprise.
3. Valid special permit granted to the ship to engage in overseas trade.
4. Valid certificate of Philippine registry of ship with IMO number.
5. Accomplished salary scale forms.

B. Under bareboat charter:
1. Letter request of the charterer including:
   i. Confirmation that the company is the bareboat charterer of the ship for enrollment;
   ii. Former name of the enterprise (if any);
   iii. Ex-name of the ship (if any).
2. Valid certificate of accreditation issued by MARINA confirming the company as an accredited maritime enterprise.
3. MARINA approval of the bareboat charter of the ship.
4. Valid certificate of Philippine registry of ship with IMO number.
5. Accomplished salary scale forms.

C. With manning agency tie-up:
1. Letter request of the manning agency including:
   i. Confirmation that the enlisted shipping enterprise is the owner/bareboat charterer of the ship for enrollment;
   ii. Former name of the shipping enterprise (if any);
   iii. Ex-name of the ship (if any).
2. Notarized manning agreement and special power of attorney between the enlisted shipping enterprise and licensed manning agency.
3. Valid certificate of accreditation issued by MARINA confirming the company as an accredited maritime enterprise.
4. Crew request from the shipping enterprise addressed to the manning agency.
5. Valid certificate of Philippine registry of the ship with IMO number.
6. Accomplished salary scale forms.
7. Additional requirements:
   i. Owned tonnage: Valid special permit granted to the ship to engage in overseas trade.
   ii. Bareboat charterer: MARINA approval of the bareboat charter of the ship.
